mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer credit balances and falling housing starts indicates a shift from durable goods to credit-fueled consumption, which historically precedes equity outperformance as corporate margins expand over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS up / HOUST down) fails to produce a positive Rank IC above 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference from the null.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth deceleration below the historical trend combined with elevated inflation expectations forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% while CPI remains sticky, or the joint signal yields a negative predictive Rank IC over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month expansion in real consumer credit balances reflects underlying household spending resilience, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The series fails to exhibit a positive monotonic relationship with forward returns, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the industrial production index signals weakening manufacturing demand and supply chain bottlenecks resolving into oversupply, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not correspond to negative equity returns, or the signal produces a positive Rank IC above 0.05 during manufacturing downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a rising federal funds rate indicates an overheating labor market that forces monetary tightening, which historically triggers equity drawdowns over a 60-day horizon as valuation multiples contract.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS adds > 200k jobs in a single month
failure_condition: Strong payroll growth combined with high interest rates fails to produce negative equity returns, or the interaction term yields a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding the long-term trend while consumer credit growth accelerates signals a credit-fueled real estate boom that historically precedes equity market corrections due to subsequent monetary tightening and margin compression over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Housing and credit booms do not precede negative equity returns, or the joint signal fails to produce a negative Rank IC below -0.03 in walk-forward testing.
